setting.note: Two Louisianas, an hour and a century apart: the Creole summer colony on Grand Isle in the Gulf, and the Pontellier house on Esplanade back in New Orleans. Chopin named the real islands, streets, and church; the bbox spans the whole run from the barrier island north to the city.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Kate Chopin, first published 1899; the author died in 1904.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States, and in the United Kingdom and the European Union, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1904).
rights.attribution: Kate Chopin, The Awakening (1899). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.accentSourceQuote: into the blue of the horizon
palette.note: The sea-blue of the Gulf horizon that draws Edna out and finally takes her, taken straight from the text. Clears WCAG AA on paper.

edna.name: Edna Pontellier
edna.colorSourceQuote: yellowish brown, about the color of her hair
